claim: Gemma 4's 31B model ranks 4th on LM Arena leaderboard while competitors at comparable quality require 4-5x more GPUs; efficiency unlocks deployment on single GPUs, phones, and edge devices
actionable: When evaluating open models, compare parameter efficiency (performance per parameter) not just absolute capability; consider Gemma 4 31B for resource-constrained deployments
source_title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind

claim: Gemma's 'Effective' models (E2B, E4B) use only 2-4B GPU memory despite having 5-10B parameters by using token mapping for non-transformer parameters; enables multi-agent workloads on consumer hardware
actionable: For mobile/edge deployment, prefer models using effective tokenization to reduce GPU memory footprint beyond parameter count
source_title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind

claim: Switching from custom Gemma license to Apache 2.0 enables sovereign institutions (Ukraine, Bulgaria, Brazil) to deploy without 18-month procurement review from legal teams
actionable: For enterprise/government adoption, prefer models licensed under Apache 2.0 or standard open licenses; custom licenses trigger legal review delays
source_title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind

claim: Thresholds for open model viability depend on task, latency requirements, hardware costs, and energy costs; matching model size to hardware and offline processing opportunities optimizes TCO
actionable: For each task, calculate total cost of ownership (upfront hardware + ongoing energy) vs. API costs; choose the model size that optimizes for your latency and cost constraints
source_title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind

claim: Fine-tuned medical variant of Gemma (MedGemma) can serve an entire hospital on 1-2 GPUs; demonstrates feasibility of domain-specific customization for enterprises with private data
actionable: For regulated domains (healthcare, finance), fine-tune open models on domain-specific private data and self-host on modest GPU clusters
source_title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind

claim: Gemma 4's multilinguality is strong enough that fine-tuning gains in additional languages diminish (<1% improvement in already-strong languages) making universal models more cost-effective than language-specific variants
actionable: Evaluate multilingual models on your target languages before investing in language-specific fine-tuning; strong base models may eliminate fine-tuning ROI
source_title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
